findings.evidence: `rg`/line check: lines 106-118 say the wrapper is exposed because `directingProbabilityMeasure_toMeasure` is an exported `rfl` coercion lemma; lines 115-128 already provide the needed `@[simp]` coercion lemma and `measurable_directingProbabilityMeasure` API.
findings.fix: Remove `@[expose]` and keep `directingProbabilityMeasure_toMeasure` as the public `@[simp]` characterization, proving it inside this module without requiring consumers to unfold the definition.
findings.issue: `directingProbabilityMeasure` is marked `@[expose]`, making the wrapper reducible across modules even though downstream only needs the measure coercion and measurability API.

summary: The bundled directing measure has the right characteristic lemmas, but the new public definition exposes its body only to make the coercion lemma definitional. That leaks implementation detail where a public rewrite lemma should carry the API.
